MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Atty. Quinton B. Cutlip of the Dolan Law Firm, Tel. No. 1-415-421-2800 appearing
for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances on the matter.
RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Pacific Bell Telephone Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first and second causes of action for general negligence and premises liability and granted as to the third cause of action for products liability. Plaintiff Kara Esborg's complaint frames the issues which need to be addressed in a summary motion. Ms. Esborg's complaint alleges: 1) that she collided with one or more low hanging wires attributable to Pacific Bell; 2) Pacific Bell negligently constructed, installed, monitored and maintained those wires; and 3) Pacific Bell negligently constructed, managed and maintained the property where those wires were attached. Pacific Bell has not satisfied its summary motion burden as to the negligence and premises liability claims. As to the first issue raised by Pacific Bell that Ms. Esborg did not collide with a wire attributable to it, the only fact proffered by Pacific Bell that negates its connection to the wire(s) which Ms. Esborg collided with is its Fact 10 that "A firefighter'said it was a Comcast cable." Ms. Esborg's hearsay objections to the portion of the first Twomey declaration and her deposition which is cited in support of Fact 10 are sustained, leaving no admissible evidence to establish Fact 10. Without any admissible evidence to support Fact 10, Pacific Bell has not satisfied its summary motion burden showing that the wire(s) Ms. Esborg collided with are not attributable to it. As to the second issue raised by Pacific Bell that it had no actual notice of any low hanging wire on the block where the incident occurred, even if this is true which it appears to be, it does not address all of Ms. Esborg's allegations why Pacific Bell is liable for negligence and premises liability and thus does not satisfy Pacific Bell's summary motion burden of showing that it was not negligent. Pacific Bell's separate statement does not proffer any facts refuting Ms. Esborg's allegations that Pacific Bell is liable for negligent construction and installation of the wire(s) she collided or negligent construction, management and maintenance of the property where those wires were attached. Ms. Esborg's objections to the new evidenced submitted with Pacific Bell's reply papers are sustained since, absent an extraordinary situation, a party against whom a summary motion is filed is entitled to at least 61 days' notice of the evidence supporting the motion before having to file opposition papers to the motion. Ms. Esborg does not oppose summary adjudication in favor of Pacific Bell on the products liability claim. Order signed. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported.=302/hk
